Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Chuah, Lukut and Bagan Pinang contests take shape
Article:
PORT DICKSON: The Chuah seat will see a straight fight in the Negri Sembilan election.
The Lukut and Bagan Pinang seats will have three-cornered contests, while Sri Tanjung will see a five-cornered contest.
The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Datuk Yew Boon Lye will defend Chuah against Pau Jeou Ching of Barisan Nasional (BN) after nominations closed.
EC returning officer Nurhazelin Makli said the nomination papers submitted by both candidates had been accepted by the commission.
"I received two nomination papers. The first was from the PH candidate at 9.02am, followed by the BN candidate at 9.06am.
"I checked all the nomination papers and none of them was rejected," she said during a brief media conference at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
Meanwhile, PH candidate for Lukut Choo Ken Hwa will defend the seat against Sathes Kumar Nilameham of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Teo Seng Li, an independent candidate.
The Bagan Pinang contest will involve a three-cornered battle, with incumbent Abd Fatah Zakaria of PN taking on PH's Nasir Rahman and Sheikh Junaidy Jamaludin of Bersatu.
In Sri Tanjung, incumbent Datuk Dr G Rajasekaran of PH will face BN's A. Achutan, M. Leevineshwaraan of Bersatu, Datuk A. Saravanan, an independent candidate, and Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, also an independent candidate.
The EC has set Aug 1 as polling day, with early voting scheduled for July 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man, 66, arrested over rape of disabled teen in Tawau
Article:
TAWAU: A 66-year-old man has been arrested and remanded for seven days over the rape of a 15-year-old girl with disabilities.
Acting Tawau police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the case came to light after the teenager told her adoptive parent that she was experiencing bodily pain.
The victim, who has attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), was taken to Tawau Hospital for a medical examination.
A report was lodged, and the suspect was arrested at a house in Kampung Ranggu.
The suspect was brought to court this morning, where a remand order was granted.
Wilynton said the suspect is being investigated under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for rape. The offence is punishable by up to 30 years' jail and whipping.
Police also seized several items believed to be connected to the case, including a collared T-shirt, a pair of jeans, a cap and other belongings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lipis route under police watch amid migrant smuggling attempts
Article:
KUANTAN: The Lipis stretch remains under close police surveillance as smuggling syndicates continue to use the route to ferry undocumented migrants from a neighbouring country into Malaysia.
Last year, police arrested 56 undocumented Myanmar nationals – 29 men and 27 women – along with eight Malaysians who acted as transporters for the migrants during a series of integrated operations in Lipis.
Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the undocumented migrants were believed to have been smuggled through Kelantan before entering Pahang.
He said that, as of June 30 this year, Lipis police had arrested two transporters and 11 undocumented migrants attempting to travel to Kuala Lumpur, believed to be the final destination for many of them.
"There has been a significant movement of undocumented migrants using the Lipis route towards Kuala Lumpur. We are aware of these activities through integrated operations (Ops Bersepadu).
"We have identified several hotspots along the Lingkaran Tengah Utama (LTU) Expressway and the trunk road. Besides regular roadblocks and enforcement operations, our operations are intelligence-led and supported by public tip-offs," he told the New Straits Times.
Azry Akmar said smugglers were likely to continue changing their tactics, including exploiting toll-free routes such as the LTU Expressway connecting Lipis and Bentong.
"The Lipis stretch is not the main smuggling corridor but rather one of several routes exploited by criminal networks to transport undocumented migrants into the country.
"Authorities remain on high alert to curb human trafficking and migrant smuggling by strengthening security at the state's border in Lipis," he said.
He urged members of the public to provide information on suspicious activities or large groups of individuals believed to be involved in migrant smuggling.
On July 6, a BMW carrying five
undocumented Myanmar migrants crashed into a water tank along Jalan Lipis-Merapoh while fleeing a police roadblock. The 45-year-old local driver, who tested positive for drugs, and the migrants were arrested.
In 2024, police arrested nine undocum nted Myanmar nationals, including a 12-year-old girl, travelling in two separate vehicles during roadblocks. In 2022, police detained 14 men crammed inside a multi-purpose vehicle in Lipis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 4 killed, 4 missing in Vietnam flash floods
Article:
HANOI: At least four people were killed and four others were missing after a flash flood swept through a mountainous village in Vietnam's northern province of Lai Chau, state media reported on Saturday.
The flood in Muong Than village early on Friday after days of heavy rains in several parts of northern Vietnam also injured seven people, reported the Vietnam News Agency.
Vietnam is prone to deadly storms and floods during the rainy season that peaks from July through September. Natural disasters, mostly floods, killed 489 people in the country last year, according to government data.
The Vietnam News Agency report had photos showing red mud and flood water blanketing the village, with rocks and wood logs piling in a corner of a damaged road.
Since Wednesday, landslides and flash floods triggered by heavy rain have also damaged roads, power grids and hundreds of houses, as well as inundating 238 hectares of crops in the region, according to the government's disaster management agency.
Several parts of northern Vietnam are forecast to receive heavy rain of up to 250mm on Saturday, the agency said, warning of more flash floods and landslides. -REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH banking on Aminuddin's MB track record in Negri polls, says Fahmi
Article:
JEMPOL: Pakatan Harapan will anchor its Negri Sembilan election campaign on its governance record and the leadership of caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said Aminuddin's administration had delivered tangible improvements in the economy, development and zakat collection.
"I believe the stability he has provided, with no scandals, no major issues and no problems throughout his eight years in office, is something we must continue.
"We also want to ensure continuity and maintain close cooperation between the state and federal governments. This will be one of our key campaign messages throughout the state election," he said.
Fahmi said this after nominations at the Jempol District Administrative Complex today.
Asked about cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, Fahmi said it would not hinder PH's efforts to retain the state.
"Our election machinery is fully prepared to defend the PH-led state government," he said.
Fahmi also urged all parties to avoid slander and refrain from exploiting issues involving race, religion and royalty.
The nomination centre at the complex covers four state constituencies — Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang and Bahau.
In Serting, PH's Yaacob Mahmood will face Fairuz Isa of PN and Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh of Bersatu.
Palong will see a three-cornered contest involving PH's Zahin Zinal Abidin, Datuk Mustapha Nagoor of BN and Bersatu's Rebin Birham.
In Jeram Padang, PH's G. Manivannan is up against Datuk Zaidy Abdul Kadir of BN, Bersatu's R. Sri Sanjeevan and Dayana Dal of Parti Asli.
Bahau will witness a straight fight between Teo Kok Seong of PH and BN's Chong Fui Ming.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police warn parties against touching sensitive issues during Negri Sembilan campaign
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Parties contesting the Negri Sembilan election have been reminded to avoid raising sensitive issues, particularly those involving religion, royalty and race (3R), throughout the campaign period.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said candidates and party supporters must refrain from making provocative, inflammatory or defamatory statements that could spark public tension, hatred or racial division.
"We urge all contesting parties, especially candidates, to focus their speeches on highlighting their respective parties' strengths without making statements that could cause public concern or anxiety," he told reporters at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
Alzafny also warned that enforcement action would be taken against candidates or supporters who breached traffic and campaign regulations, including riding motorcycles without helmets during election-related activities.
He said all party representatives had been briefed on the dos and don'ts during the campaign period, adding that enforcement would be carried out in accordance with existing laws.
Meanwhile, Alzafny said the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election proceeded smoothly, with all eight nomination centres completing the process without any major issues.
He said the police's main priority was to ensure the nomination process was conducted safely and without any untoward incidents.
"Based on feedback received, the situation at all eight nomination centres was under control.
"Party supporters also complied with the instructions and advice given by the police," he said.
He added that the situation at all nomination centres remained calm and orderly from early morning, with supporters dispersing immediately after returning officers announced the list of candidates.
A total of 2,373 police officers and personnel were deployed across all nomination centres to manage security, maintain public order and control traffic.
Alzafny said police observations showed that the number of supporters at each nomination centre ranged from 1,000 to 2,500.
Jempol recorded the highest turnout, with about 2,500 supporters, while Kuala Pilah recorded the lowest, with around 1,000.
He added that police had not received any reports of incidents related to the nomination process.
"We would like to thank everyone, especially party supporters, for maintaining order, harmony and complying with all regulations and instructions issued," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN unfazed by three-cornered fight in Juasseh
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for Juasseh Datuk Ismail Lasim is unfazed by the three-cornered contest for the seat in the Negri Sembilan election.
Ismail said he respected the decision of all candidates to offer themselves to the voters and was ready to face the contest.
"Juasseh will see a three-cornered fight and, for me, this is normal in a democratic process.
"Everyone has the right to offer themselves as candidates," he said after the nomination process today.
Ismail will face Pakatan Harapan's Mohd Aidil Abdullah and Bersatu's Mohd Zuhaimi Md Yusof.
The former Negri Sembilan executive councillor said he was delighted to return to contest Juasseh, having previously represented the constituency in 2018 before moving to Senaling in 2023.
"Based on the party's needs, I have returned to Juasseh and I'm happy because this is my hometown.
"I am grateful to once again have the opportunity to offer my services to the people of my hometown.
"I hope to receive their support so I can continue the work I began during my previous term. There were matters I did not have the chance to complete, and now I have been given another opportunity," he said.
On his priorities if elected, Ismail said he would focus on serving the people while addressing the needs of a rapidly developing constituency.
He said Juasseh was among the fastest-growing areas in the Kuala Pilah parliamentary constituency, with increasing housing developments expected to bring a larger population and new challenges.
"I have yet to study all the issues in depth, but Juasseh is a developing area. With more housing projects and population growth, there will certainly be challenges.
"Having previously served as the state executive councillor for education, I will focus on the people's welfare as well as education for the community and children in Juasseh," he said.
Ismail retained the Senaling seat in 2023 after defeating Bersatu's Amrina Mohammad Khalid.
He secured 3,724 votes against Amrina's 3,062, giving him a majority of 662 votes.
Having moved to Juasseh, Ismail will replace Bibi Sharliza Mohd Khalid, who won the seat in 2023 with a slim majority of 78 votes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man, 72, arrested for drink-driving after Manjung crash
Article:
MANJUNG: A 72-year-old man was arrested last night for driving under the influence of alcohol following a two-vehicle accident at Jalan Lumut-Sitiawan here.
The 11.20pm incident involved a Mercedes-Benz driven by the man and a Honda driven by a woman.
A viral video showed a heated exchange breaking out between the two drivers after the accident, before police arrived at the scene and detained the man.
It is understood that the man had emerged from a junction and the woman, who was travelling behind him, was unable to stop in time and crashed into the rear of his vehicle.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Hasbullah Abd Rahman said the driver was taken to the police station for a breathalyser test.
"The man was later released on police bail pending completion of investigations. No injuries were reported in the incident," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 45A of the Road Transport Act for driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs.
Upon conviction, offenders face a fine of between RM1,000 and RM6,000 and imprisonment for up to one year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz to new Johor exco: No honeymoon, time to deliver
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Menteri Besar Onn Hafiz Ghazi has told his new state executive council (Exco) lineup that there will be no honeymoon period following their appointment today.
He said everyone must get to work immediately to deliver on the mandate given by the people and realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda.
"There is no honeymoon vacation," Onn Hafiz told a press conference at his official residence.
"The mandate given by Bangsa Johor must be translated into harder work, more efficient delivery, and results that can be felt by every citizen."
He said the state administration's priority is to implement the Maju Johor 2030 agenda to build a more developed and prosperous Johor.
The new lineup, he added, combines experienced leaders and new faces to ensure the state government remains strong, efficient, and responsive.
Onn Hafiz also expressed his gratitude to the Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim for consenting to the appointments.
He said his administration would focus on improving public service delivery, accelerating policy implementation, and ensuring development benefits all communities and levels of society.
Earlier, the
10 Exco members
took their oaths of office before Tunku Ismail during a ceremony at Istana Bukit Serene.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: BN-PN cooperation based on understanding, not formal pact
Article:
REMBAU: The cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negri Sembilan election is an understanding, and not an agreement or pact, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said.
The BN chairman said the coalition believed that through the tactical move, with BN contesting 25 seats and PN 11 seats, BN would be able to regain control of Negri Sembilan.
"We will ensure that the coordination carried out is not an agreement or a pact, but an understanding.
"This understanding is important for us because previously BN only won 14 seats.
"Insya-Allah, with the tactical move we have taken, Negri Sembilan will once again be governed by BN.
"The blue wave has arrived in Negri Sembilan, insya-Allah," he said.
He said this in a speech to BN supporters after accompanying BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, who is also the BN candidate for the Rantau  seat, at the nomination centre at Dewan Sri Rembau here today.
Last Wednesday, BN announced 25 out of the 36 candidates representing the coalition in the state election.
The following day, PN announced its candidates to fill the 11 seats, comprising five candidates from Pas, four from Wawasan, and one each from Gerakan and Parti Rakyat India Malaysia.
At the same time, Zahid also urged BN machinery members to work hard to ensure victory for Mohamad, better known as Tok Mat, with a higher majority, as well as for BN candidates in the Chembong and Kota seats.
"Hopefully, with a new approach and spirit, and the arrival of the blue wave, we pray and work towards achieving a higher majority.
"We respect the institution of customs and the Rulers, and we uphold them. Insya-Allah Tok Mat, we will not break through the roof, we will build a new roof," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin admits tough battle ahead in BN stronghold Linggi
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said his chances of winning the Linggi seat in the state election remained "50-50".
The Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, however, expressed confidence that voters would support the coalition's efforts to ensure a stable government.
"At the moment, I still see the chances as 50-50. There is still time. I cannot say I am 100 per cent confident, but the opportunity to win is 50-50," he said when asked about his chances after submitting his nomination papers at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
Aminuddin acknowledged that contesting the Linggi seat was a difficult decision, as the constituency has long been regarded as a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold, with PH never having won the seat.
Rejecting claims that he had "run away" from his previous state seat of Sikamat, Aminuddin, who is also Negri Sembilan PKR chairman, said his move was aimed at helping the coalition win new constituencies needed to form a stable government.
"I did not run away. I left Sikamat because PH needs to win new seats to ensure a stable government," he said.
Aminuddin, who is also Port Dickson member of Parliament, said contesting the Linggi seat would enable him to further strengthen development efforts and improve the welfare of residents in the constituency.
"It is not easy because I am leaving a constituency where I had a comfortable majority," he said, referring to Sikamat.
He said he was aware that he was contesting not only against his opponent, but also BN's election machinery.
Aminuddin said Negri Sembilan had much to be proud of, including its development achievements, human capital progress and racial harmony, which he described as the state's greatest strength.
Asked about PH's overall target, he said the coalition was aiming to win more than 18 seats in the state election.
"That is our hope, our aspiration and what we are working towards," he said.
The Linggi seat will see a three-cornered contest in the election.
Earlier, returning officer Nurhazelin Makli said the Election Commission had accepted the nomination papers of all three candidates.
Incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli of BN will defend the seat against Aminuddin and Bersatu's Datuk Zamri Md Said in the Aug 1 election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN confident understanding with BN can deliver Negri Sembilan victory
Article:
SEREMBAN: Perikatan Nasional (PN) is confident that its understanding with Barisan Nasional (BN), which has created a 'blue wave', can capture Negri Sembilan in the state election.
Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin said the confidence was based on strong support for the alliance on social media.
"Our feeling as a group within PN heading into the Negri Sembilan election is different compared with Johor.
"We can already see a stronger sentiment towards achieving victory in Negri Sembilan because this time PN is not only placing our candidates strategically in certain state seats, but BN is also not fielding candidates in areas where we are contesting, and vice versa.
"Over the past two days, I have seen on social media that people feel this is the moment they have been waiting for," he said.
He said this to reporters while accompanying PN candidates at the nomination centre at the Seremban City Council building in Forest Heights here today.
Also present was Pas vice-president Datuk Idris Ahmad.
Hamzah said the coalition also wanted BN's victory in Johor to be repeated in Negri Sembilan.
"If BN was able to form the government in Johor, it shows that giving support to BN can lead to victory, and that is something we also want.
"In Negri Sembilan, we want to take over the state government. Insya-Allah, we can achieve a big victory with the same objective.
"BN and PN are blue in colour, while PH and Bersatu are red. I believe the wave this time is blue, going against the red tide."
Meanwhile, Idris said the Negri Sembilan election would be the beginning of a bigger victory in the next general election.
"Johor was described as the seed. Here in Negri Sembilan it is beginning to blossom, and Insya-Allah, the fruit will grow in Putrajaya during the next general election.
"This is a beginning after Johor, and there is a rise in support among the people.
"Looking at comments on social media, almost 90 per cent of netizens agree with this alliance, which is also what they want."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 103 candidates confirmed for 36-seat Negri Sembilan election
Article:
SEREMBAN: A total of 103 candidates from various political parties and independent candidates will contest the 36 seats up for grabs in the Negri Sembilan election.
Of the total seats, 21 will see three-cornered contests in the state election, which has been fixed for polling on Aug 1.
Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said all 103 nomination papers received were accepted, with no candidates disqualified after the nomination process at eight nomination centres statewide concluded today.
He said of the total, Pakatan Harapan (PH) is fielding 36 candidates, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 25 candidates, Bersatu with 24 candidates and Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 11 candidates.
Meanwhile, Berjasa, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) are each fielding one candidate, while there are four independent candidates.
"Overall, 11 seats will see straight fights, 21 seats will see three-cornered contests, two seats will have four-cornered contests and another two seats will see five-cornered contests," he said at a press conference at Wisma Persekutuan here.
Ramlan said of the total number of candidates contesting, 94 are men while nine are women.
He said based on age categories, three candidates are aged between 18 and 29, 12 candidates are aged between 30 and 39, 39 candidates are aged between 40 and 49, 33 candidates are aged between 50 and 59, while 16 candidates are aged 60 and above.
"The oldest candidate is PH's Gemencheh state seat candidate Abd Latif A Tambi, aged 70, while the youngest is Bersatu's Sri Tanjung candidate Leevineshwaran Murugan, aged 23," he said.
He said the official campaign period began immediately after the nomination process ended today and will continue until 11.59pm on July 31.
Ramlan added that the EC had established 36 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams to monitor all campaign activities throughout the period.
He also reminded all candidates to comply with laws and regulations concerning campaign materials and avoid using materials containing elements of incitement, racial issues or those touching on religious sensitivities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor exco line-up unveiled with four new faces
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Four new faces have been appointed to the Johor state executive council (Exco), while six existing members have been retained.
The new members are Md Israk Abdullah (Kukup), Dr Mohd Naqib Ghazali (Panti), Hasrunizah Hassan (Pulai Sebatang), and P. Pannir Selvam (Perling).
Md Israk takes over the Agriculture, Agro-based Industry and Rural Development portfolio, while Dr Mohd Naqib handles Education and Information.
Hasrunizah will manage Women, Family and Community Development, and Pannir Selvam will lead Unity, Heritage and Culture.
The four replace Khairin-Nisa Ismail (Women, Family and Community Development), Datuk Zahari Sarip (Agriculture, Agro-based Industry and Rural Development), Aznan Tamin (Education and Information), and K. Raven Kumar (Unity, Heritage and Culture).
Out of the four replaced members, only Khairin-Nisa did not defend her Serom state seat in the state election.
Meanwhile, the six Exco members who retained their portfolios are Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor (Housing and Local Government), Mohd Hairi Mad Shah (Youth and Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives), and Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid (Islamic Religious Affairs).
Also retained are Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh (Works, Transportation and Infrastructure), Lee Ting Han (Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources), and Ling Tian Soon (Health and Environment).
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the new line-up at a press conference at his official residence in Saujana today.
Earlier, the line-up took their oaths of office before the Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Serene.
The ceremony was attended by Onn Hafiz, Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Dr Rahim Ramli, and State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir.
On Sunday, Onn Hafiz, who is Machap assemblyman, was sworn in as Johor Menteri Besar for a second consecutive term after Barisan Nasional won the state election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Earthquakes on the rise? Experts say no, but preparedness remains key
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Reports of earthquakes have become an almost daily occurrence, with powerful tremors striking different parts of the world and raising concerns over whether global seismic activity is increasing.
While some quakes cause little damage, others have claimed thousands of lives and levelled entire cities within seconds.
Most recently, two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24 killed 4,734 people and injured 16,740 others.
In recent months, strong earthquakes have also hit countries along the Pacific Ring of Fire, including the Philippines, Indonesia, Japan, Chile and Tonga, prompting questions about whether Malaysia, which lies close to one of the world's most active seismic zones, is at greater risk.
The Venezuela disaster, involving earthquakes measuring magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 near La Guaira and Caracas, was the deadliest of the year. Hundreds of buildings were destroyed, more than 17,000 people were displaced and over one million residents were affected.
Within the past week alone, earthquakes exceeding magnitude 5.0 were recorded in Papua New Guinea, Chile, Russia's Kuril Islands, Tonga, Indonesia's Banda Sea and Alaska.
Although most caused little damage, they underscore the continued activity along the world's tectonic plate boundaries.
Pacific Ring of Fire remains the world's most active seismic zone
Nearly 90 per cent of the world's earthquakes occur along the 40,000km Pacific Ring of Fire, which stretches from New Zealand through Papua New Guinea, Indonesia, the Philippines, Japan, Russia and Alaska before reaching the western coasts of North and South America.
The region marks the convergence of several tectonic plates, making it prone to frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions. Indonesia, the Philippines and Japan remain among the world's most earthquake-prone countries.
Despite growing public concern, international seismologists said the increase in earthquake reports does not mean the Earth is entering an abnormal phase.
According to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), tectonic plate movement is a natural process that has been taking place for millions of years.
"Experts have explained that a major earthquake in one country does not trigger another major earthquake thousands of kilometres away.
"Instead, the rise in reports is largely due to advances in monitoring technology, which can now detect almost every moderate tremor worldwide within minutes."
MetMalaysia said the death toll from earthquakes depends not only on magnitude but also on preparedness, earthquake-resistant building standards, population density and the effectiveness of emergency response.
Malaysia is close to, but outside, the Ring of Fire
Although Malaysia is not located on the Pacific Ring of Fire, it lies close to the highly active seismic zones of Indonesia and the Philippines.
"Sabah is only a few hundred kilometres from the southern Philippines, while the western coast of Peninsular Malaysia faces Sumatra, one of the world's most active seismic regions.
"As a result, major earthquakes in Indonesia and the Philippines are often felt in Sabah, Sarawak and occasionally Peninsular Malaysia."
Sabah remains the country's most earthquake-prone state because of its active fault lines. The magnitude 6.0 Ranau earthquake in 2015, which claimed 18 lives, demonstrated that Malaysia is not immune.
Since then, hundreds of minor tremors have been recorded annually in Sabah, although most go unnoticed.
Following the Ranau earthquake and the 2004 Aceh tsunami, Malaysia strengthened its preparedness through MetMalaysia's round-the-clock seismic monitoring network and tsunami warning system. Modern developments, particularly in Sabah, are also designed to meet earthquake-resistant standards.
However, experts said many older buildings still require reassessment, while public awareness of basic earthquake safety measures such as "Drop, Cover and Hold On" remains low.
They stressed that no technology can accurately predict when or where an earthquake will occur. Instead, countries should focus on strengthening early warning systems, enforcing building standards and improving public preparedness.
For Malaysia, while the threat is lower than in Japan or Indonesia, its proximity to the Pacific Ring of Fire means the risk cannot be ignored. Recent disasters from Mindanao to Venezuela serve as a reminder that the best protection against earthquakes is preparedness.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari: BN-PN cooperation depends on common ground, not ideology
Article:
TAMPIN: Any cooperation or understanding between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) will depend on both sides' willingness to find common ground despite their differing political ideologies.
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said ideological differences were among the main factors contributing to the different positions taken by political parties.
However, he said if there was room to reach an understanding in certain areas or states, the matter would depend on the consideration of the respective parties.
"In politics, every party has its own ideology. Differences in views often arise because the ideologies they uphold are also different.
"If both sides are able to reach an understanding and there are areas or states where they can contest together, that is up to them," he said.
He was speaking to reporters when asked about the seat allocation arrangement between BN and PN for the Negri Sembilan election today.
Johari, however, stressed that under the democratic system, the final decision remained in the hands of voters.
"Ultimately, it is not leaders who decide, but the people who make the decision."
It was previously reported that BN would contest 25 of the 36 seats in Negri Sembilan, while the remaining 11 seats would be contested by PN following seat allocation discussions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalaluddin faces three-way Pertang fight, Loke in Chennah duel
Article:
JELEBU: Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias and Anthony Loke are set to defend their respective seats in the Negri Sembilan election, with the former facing a three-cornered contest and the latter a straight fight.
The official candidate line-up was announced by Jelebu returning officer Abdul Rahim A. Aziz at the nomination centre inside Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang today.
For the Pertang seat, Jalaluddin, who is also state Umno liaison committee chairman, will face a three-cornered contest against Pakatan Harapan's (PH) Mohd Umry Abdul Khois and Bersatu's Mohd Faizal Fadli Mohd Idrus.
Meanwhile, in Chennah, Loke, who is DAP secretary-general, will go head-to-head with BN candidate Siow Kong Choon.
For the Klawang seat, PH's Datuk Bakri Sawir will defend his seat in a three-cornered contest against his cousin Danni Rais, who is representing Perikatan Nasional (PN), and Bersatu candidate Muhammad Adib Musa.
In the Sungai Lui seat, BN candidate Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali will seek to defend the constituency he has held for three terms, facing challenges from PH's Zainal Fikri Abd Kadir and Bersatu's Mazrulhisham Abd Mansor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri polls will test claims DAP is losing support, says Loke
Article:
JELEBU: Claims of declining support for DAP will ultimately be determined by the outcome of the Negri Sembilan election, says party secretary-general Anthony Loke.
"The reality is that every election is different," he told reporters after nominations in Kuala Klawang.
"Whether support has declined, remained the same or increased depends entirely on the decision made by voters on Aug 1."
Loke will be defending his Chennah seat in a straight fight against Barisan Nasional's Siow Kong Choon.
Separately, Loke said the state seats within the Jelebu parliamentary constituency are crucial to Pakatan Harapan's efforts to form the next state government.
"In 2018, we succeeded in forming the state government because of the contribution from the two seats in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency, namely Chennah and Klawang.
"So, if we want to once again form the state government on our own, then the seats in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency will certainly be critical contributors," he said.
Loke said he had anticipated that the opposition would adopt a one-on-one contest strategy in Chennah.
"We will campaign vigorously based on our track record and the work we have done in this constituency," he said.
The Seremban member of parliament described Chennah as a mixed constituency with no single ethnic group forming a majority.
"To win this seat and secure a mandate from the voters, you need support from all communities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Keep Adat Perpatih out of politics, candidates and parties told
Article:
REMBAU: Parties and candidates contesting in the Negri Sembilan polls should stay away from politicising issues related to the state's unique Adat Perpatih customary system during campaigning.
"We do not touch 'adat', we must respect it," said Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan.
"Do not mix 'adat' with politics because it will complicate matters.
"We do not need to use issues related to 'adat' campaign material," he told a press conference after nominations.
Negri Sembilan has seen a royal dispute between the Yang di-Pertuan Besar with four Undang, two of whom have since been replaced.
Mohamad, who is BN's candidate for Rantau, vowed a healthy campaign in Negri Sembilan.
"We want to present the BN manifesto and explain to the people why they need to return the mandate to BN," he said.
Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, is seeking to defend the seat he first won in 2004.
In the last election, Mohamad retained the seat against Perikatan Nasional's Rozmal Malakan with a 10,280-vote majority.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Senior citizen withdraws from Sikamat race over incomplete documents
Article:
SEREMBAN: A 75-year-old man's hopes of contesting as an independent candidate in the Negri Sembilan election were dashed after he was forced to withdraw from the race.
Bujang Abu, who had applied to contest the Sikamat seat, was said to have failed to complete the documents required by the Election Commission (EC).
The retired civil servant had previously contested the same seat in the 2013 General Election and the 2023 state election, but lost both contests.
Bujang said although he was disappointed, he acknowledged that it was his own mistake for failing to complete the required documents before today's nomination process.
"During the nomination process earlier, I was informed that I had not submitted my expenditure statement from the 2023 state election.
"I should have submitted the statement to the EC after the election.
"After checking with the EC, I am satisfied that it was indeed my mistake for not submitting the statement after the previous election, although I am slightly disappointed," he said when met at the nomination centre at Wisma MBS here this morning.
Bujang, who now spends much of his time performing street music to earn extra income, said he would try his luck in future elections.
"Let's see, if I am still around, I will contest again in the future. For this time, I am unable to contest.
"I have contested in the Sikamat seat in 2013 and 2023. My aim was to serve the people of Sikamat and bring changes to the area," said Bujang, who arrived using a walking stick.
The Sikamat seat was previously held by Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, who won it for four consecutive terms since 2008.
However, Aminuddin has moved to contest the Linggi seat in this election.
Sikamat will see a three-cornered contest involving the Menteri Besar's political secretary, Nor Azman Mohamad of Pakatan Harapan, Datuk Razali Abu Samah of Perikatan Nasional and Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari urges BN machinery to run healthy Negri Sembilan polls campaign
Article:
TAMPIN: Barisan Nasional (BN) election machinery members and supporters have been urged to conduct a healthy campaign throughout the Negri Sembilan election period.
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said this was to ensure the democratic process during the state election campaign period took place in a harmonious atmosphere.
He said an election was not merely a platform for political competition, but also an avenue for the people to choose capable leaders who could bring development to the state and local communities.
"We have two BN candidates in the Tampin parliamentary constituency, namely in the Gemencheh and Repah state seats.
"I hope all machinery members can campaign in a healthy manner and go down to meet the people to ensure both candidates are given the mandate," he said this when met after the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election at Dewan Perdana Tampin today.
He said both BN candidates had good service records and were believed to be capable of contributing to the state's development if elected.
He said Gemencheh candidate Suhaimizan Bizar had experience as an elected representative and intended to continue serving the community, while Repah candidate Koh Kim Swee was seen as having a good approach in engaging with people from various ethnic backgrounds.
"Our aim is to bring forward leaders with calibre who can work together to help the people, especially in the Tampin parliamentary constituency," he said.
On the understanding between BN and Perikatan Nasional during the polls, Johari said the matter was reached through an agreement between both coalitions.
"Every political party has its own ideology. If there is room to reach an understanding and cooperate in any area or state, it is up to the parties involved.
"In the end, it is not leaders who determine victory, but the people who make the decision," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No quick fix: Expert warns against diabetes jabs for weight loss
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The growing trend of diabetes medication injections for weight-loss without medical supervision carries serious health risks, while depriving diabetic patients of essential medicine, an expert has warned.
Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia medical lecturer and consultant physician Dr Mohd Firdaus A. Bakar said the off-label use of diabetes medication solely for weight loss offered no proven clinical benefit for obesity or diabetes.
He said taking the medication without assessment and monitoring by a qualified doctor exposed users to health risks because the drugs affect hormones and metabolism.
"It is not a supplement or an ordinary weight-loss product. The side effects are real and include nausea, vomiting, drowsiness and gastrointestinal problems," Dr Firdaus said.
"In certain cases, users may also develop pancreatitis, gallstones and dehydration," he said.
He said the risks were even greater for those with underlying medical conditions who might not be suitable candidates for the injections. Rapid weight loss through these injections could also reduce muscle mass and worsen nutritional deficiencies.
Firdaus' warning follows social media reports of a doctor who shared the case of a 24-year-old woman preparing for marriage who was hospitalised after suffering serious complications from weight-loss injections.
The woman sought treatment at the doctor's clinic in Batu Pahat, Johor, after experiencing severe abdominal pain, jaundice and a rapid heartbeat of between 132 and 140 beats per minute.
She had reportedly lost 26kg, dropping from 80kg to 54kg, before being referred to Johor Bahru Hospital. Doctors there found she had developed liver inflammation, swelling and intestinal leakage.
Commenting on the increasing popularity of the injections, Dr Firdaus said the medication had become a common topic in clinics and on social media platforms.
He said the trend was worrying because it created a false impression that healthy lifestyle changes could be replaced by weekly injections.
"The proper approach is to change eating habits and increase physical activity. If they stop taking the injections, their weight will most likely return to previous levels," he said.
Dr Firdaus said that obesity was not merely an issue of calorie intake but involved psychological, socioeconomic, environmental and cultural factors that required comprehensive, long-term treatment.
"If we raise a generation that believes every metabolic problem can be solved with an injection, we risk creating long-term dependency," he said.
For diabetes patients, he said stable treatment and proper medical supervision remained essential to prevent serious complications such as stroke and kidney failure.
"Prescription medication should only be used by the right patients under close medical supervision. "The goal is not simply to become slim, but to achieve overall, sustainable health," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's ancient fault lines showing signs of renewed activity, says expert
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ancient fault lines in Malaysia are showing signs of more frequent movement and energy release.
Among the clearest indicators is the Mersing Fault in Johor, which has been identified as becoming active again after more than a century without any recorded seismic activity in the area.
Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) Faculty of Science and Technology senior lecturer in the Geology Programme, Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences, Associate Professor Dr Mohd Rozi Umor, said a series of tremors in Segamat – including a magnitude 4.1 main quake followed by aftershocks measuring magnitudes 2.8 and 3.2 – as well as a magnitude 3.3 tremor in Batu Pahat point to continued movement along the same fault system within a relatively short period.
He said the activity represents a process of tectonic energy readjustment within ancient faults as the Earth's crust seeks to regain stability, influenced by the extension of the Mersing Fault.
"Historical records show that Johor experienced stronger earthquakes in 1922, measuring magnitudes 5.4 and 5.0.
"This shows that fault activity in Peninsular Malaysia is not a new phenomenon. It is simply being recorded more frequently than before," he told Harian Metro.
Mohd Rozi said current data and research indicate signs of minor reactivation along certain fault lines, but stressed that this does not mean the country's fault systems as a whole are becoming significantly more active.
He said the Department of Minerals and Geoscience Malaysia (JMG) mapped 11 active fault zones nationwide between 2016 and 2020, six of which are located in Peninsular Malaysia, namely Bukit Tinggi in Pahang, Bok Bak in Kedah and Perlis, Kuala Pilah in Negri Sembilan, and Mersing in Johor.
"The tremors in Segamat are believed to have originated from the Mersing Fault, which has been identified as becoming active again despite there previously being no recorded seismic events in the area.
"This indicates that these ancient fault lines still have the potential to move or become active again.
"However, that potential does not mean such activity will occur in the immediate future," he said.
Mohd Rozi said there is no conclusive evidence that Malaysia's earthquake risk is increasing. Instead, improvements in monitoring technology have made earthquakes appear more frequent because more events are now being detected.
He said the seismic sensor networks operated by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) and JMG are now far more extensive than they were 10 to 20 years ago.
Malaysia currently has about 47 passive seismic stations operating across the country, compared with a much smaller network previously.
As a result, minor tremors measuring between magnitudes 2.0 and 3.0, which previously went undetected, are now recorded and publicly reported.
He said Malaysia's monitoring system is adequate, with MetMalaysia actively monitoring seismic activity and issuing real-time updates, including regional earthquakes such as the recent magnitude 5.9 quake northeast of Taiwan and the magnitude 5.2 quake south of Java, both of which were reported within minutes together with tsunami threat assessments.
"In terms of monitoring local fault lines, the 47 passive stations are operating across various states, while the Malaysian Seismic Hazard Map is currently being updated in collaboration with JMG and local universities.
"The existing system performs well in detecting distant earthquakes and issuing regional tsunami warnings. However, sensor coverage for smaller fault systems in Peninsular Malaysia is still being expanded.
"Malaysia also maintains regional cooperation through seismic data-sharing with Indonesia's BMKG and the Philippines' PHIVOLCS, coordination of regional tsunami warning systems under the Asean/Unesco Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission (IOC) framework, and exchanges of technical expertise."
Mohd Rozi said future developments in geological monitoring that Malaysia should consider include denser seismic sensor networks, artificial intelligence (AI) models capable of detecting early seismic patterns, and direct integration of earthquake alerts to mobile phones, similar to the ShakeAlert system in the United States and Japan's Earthquake Early Warning (EEW) system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin faces three-cornered battle in Linggi
Article:
PORT DICKSON: The Linggi seat is set for a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election, with the incumbent, caretaker menteri besar and a former contender for the seat set to face off in a closely watched battle.
The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli of Barisan Nasional (BN) will take on caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Datuk Zamri Md Said of Bersatu after nominations closed at 10am.
Returning officer Nurhazelin Makli said the commission had accepted the nomination papers submitted by all three candidates.
"I received three nomination papers. The first was from the PH candidate at 9.04am, followed by the Bersatu candidate at 9.10am and the BN candidate at 9.15am.
"I checked all the nomination papers and none of them were rejected," she said during a brief media conference at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
The Linggi contest is expected to be among the most closely watched battles in the election, given Aminuddin's roles as caretaker menteri besar and Negri Sembilan PH and PKR chairman, while the seat is widely regarded as a BN stronghold.
Aminuddin had represented Sikamat since 2008 before switching to contest the Linggi seat.
In the last state election, Faizal won the seat with 7,832 votes, while Zamri received 6,371 votes, giving Faizal a majority of 1,461 votes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Egypt's 'Garbage City' recyclers reap gains from Iran war plastic squeeze
Article:
CAIRO, (Egypt): In the labyrinthine alleyways of Cairo's Garbage City, recycling specialist Peter Romany finds himself fielding calls from factories scrambling for plastic to plug supply shortfalls caused by the Iran war.
The 25-year-old is among the hundreds of recyclers and manufacturers across Egypt benefiting from a war-driven surge in demand ever since the United States and Iran choked off the Strait of Hormuz — a major shipping lane for the raw materials from which plastic is made.
At the heart of the boom is the sprawling eastern Cairo settlement of Manshiyet Nasser, where generations of garbage collectors have built one of the world's most sophisticated informal recycling systems.
"Before the war, we were the ones calling factories, trying to sell our material," Romany told AFP, standing beside towering bales of compressed plastic.
"But after the war broke out, the factories started calling us. They'd ask: How much do you have? Can you deliver today? That never used to happen."
- Built on trash -
Home to more than 115,000 residents, Manshiyet Nasser is a predominantly Coptic Christian neighbourhood nestled beneath the Mokattam hill and facing Cairo's historic Citadel.
The settlement handles more than a third of the capital's waste, according to government figures.
Families live and work under the same roof, often separated from mountains of waste by little more than a staircase or curtain, exposing them to foul odours, plastic fumes and other health risks.
Downstairs, men sort plastics, cardboard, paper, metals and glass into neat piles destined for workshops and factories.
Upstairs, children pore over schoolbooks, mothers prepare lunch and television sets flicker in cramped living rooms, all against the constant background noise of shredders whining and baling presses pounding below.
The smell of rubbish hangs heavy in the air as pickup trucks and handcarts crawl through narrow alleyways, unloading the day's collections while children weave between them chasing footballs.
It's a well-oiled machine, kicked into overdrive by a war more than a thousand kilometres away.
- Cash upfront -
Romany specialises in recycled polyethylene, one of the world's most widely used plastics and a key ingredient in packaging.
According to the pricing agency Independent Commodity Intelligence Services (ICIS), the Middle East is a major global supplier of polyethylene, with around 85 per cent of its exports passing through the strait.
Egypt imports around 40 per cent of its raw plastic materials, mainly from Gulf countries, Europe, China and South Korea, according to the Chamber of Chemical Industries.
Packaging and plastic prices have more than doubled for some products, three industry sources told AFP, pushing manufacturers towards locally recycled alternatives.
Factories that would normally delay payment started putting down cash upfront "because they were so eager to secure material", said Rizq Yousif, who mainly recycles PET, the plastic widely used in beverage and food packaging.
Yousif told AFP demand had tripled, while prices for some recycled plastics increased by up to 60 per cent.
- A temporary boom? -
The disruption has been good for local business all along the value chain.
"We have been in this business for 16 years," said Fayrouz El-Sayed, chief executive of Sadat City Chemical Fibre Factory, which produces polyester fibres from used plastic bottles.
But only since the latest crisis have they managed to crack new markets as far away as Brazil, she said.
Nesma El-Areef, senior marketing and sales manager at Uflex Egypt, which converts plastic waste into new packaging materials, said demand for the company's recycled products rose by up to 40 percent.
"We saw a significant increase in orders, particularly from food and beverage manufacturers, because we offered a more readily available alternative to imported materials," she told AFP.
Despite the gains, industry figures believe the boom could fade once supply routes stabilise.
Yousif said prices and demand began easing shortly after US President Donald Trump announced last month that negotiations with Iran were progressing.
"Just one post dropped the market. After the war, I am not sure this will last," he said.
But this week, Trump said the US was reinstating its blockade of Iranian ports and "taking over" the Strait of Hormuz as fresh fighting between the two countries flared.
Orders have already picked back up, according to both Romany and Yousif.
"We're used to it by now," Yousif said.
"Whenever there's trouble there, the customers start calling us." — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KWAP pursues recovery of RM163.4 million eFishery investment after fraud scandal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) it is continuing to pursue all available avenues to maximise the recovery of its investment in eFishery.
In a statement today, KWAP said its total investment in eFishery amounted to RM163.4 million, representing approximately 2.51 per cent of the company's total shareholding.
KWAP was a minority shareholder, while the majority of the company's shares were held by other investors, including major global institutional investors that were similarly affected by the misconduct.
"In light of the matters raised, we wish to provide further clarity on the actions undertaken following our internal review, as well as the measures being implemented to safeguard the interests of the fund.
"Following the discovery of the irregularities, KWAP conducted an internal investigation and the necessary reviews of the investment processes, post-investment monitoring arrangements and information available to KWAP throughout the investment period," it said.
The Fund also said appropriate follow-up actions have been taken in accordance with KWAP's internal governance and accountability framework.
KWAP said the broader eFishery case involved deliberate financial manipulation and misrepresentation of the company's financial reporting.
On April 29, 2026, eFishery co-founder and former chief executive officer Gibran Huzaifah was sentenced by the Bandung District Court to nine years' imprisonment following his conviction for embezzlement and money laundering.
KWAP also said it has further strengthened its approach to private markets investing.
"The measures include greater portfolio diversification, investing alongside experienced fund managers and strategic partners, enhanced post-investment monitoring, and closer oversight of material developments involving portfolio companies.
"The Fund remains supported by a diversified portfolio across asset classes, sectors and geographies," it said.
Based on unaudited results for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025, KWAP recorded gross investment income of RM8.33 billion, with total funds under management of RM195.26 billion.
KWAP remains committed to managing the Fund prudently, transparently and responsibly in fulfilment of its statutory mandate to assist the Government in meeting its pension obligations to public sector retirees.
Last Thursday, the Finance Ministry in a written reply published on the Parliament website said it found KWAP was deceived over its investment in eFishery and that it was a well-planned fraud. (hyperlink: https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1489970/kwap-duped-investing-nearly-rm200mil-efishery-says-pm-watch)
The ministry said the Indonesian aquaculture startup's management had manipulated its financial statements.
Following the discovery, the ministry said the consortium of investors, including KWAP, had taken firm measures, including legal action, fund recovery efforts, internal governance reviews and strengthened controls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Felda must continue to be strengthened to uplift settlers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) must continue to be strengthened as an institution tasked with uplifting the dignity of settlers and their families, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
The Prime Minister said he stressed this during a courtesy call by new Felda chairman Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd Zahir in Putrajaya yesterday.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the welfare of every generation of settlers must go hand in hand with efforts to strengthen the economic competitiveness and sustainability of Felda, including giving special attention to issues faced by the younger generation.
"I am confident that Ahmad Badri, with the responsibility entrusted to him, will be able to continue the agenda of restoring and strengthening Felda.
"In fact, the Madani Government will also continue to provide full support to ensure that this institution, which serves the people, continues to progress and remains relevant for future generations," he said in a Facebook post today.
Ahmad Badri was appointed Felda chairman effective July 15 to replace Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek, whose term of service ended on June 30. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Body of man attacked by crocodile found with bite marks
Article:
KINABATANGAN: The body of a 57-year-old man who was attacked by a crocodile while cleaning a boat at Kampung Batu Putih yesterday has been found.
Rusli Mardin was found at 4.45am by villagers, about 500m downstream from where he was last seen.
Kinabatangan Fire and Rescue Department chief Suriyadi Darwis said villagers alerted firefighters after discovering the body.
"The victim's body was intact, but there were bite marks on his abdomen and the right side of his neck," he said when contacted.
In the 6.30pm incident yesterday, Rusli was
attacked and dragged
away by the reptile while cleaning his boat in the river. His son witnessed the attack.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tun Faisal faces ex-Melaka deputy police chief in Sikamat three-way fight
Article:
SEREMBAN: The Sikamat seat will see a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election.
The announcement of the candidates was made by Seremban mayor Datuk Masri Baharudin, who is also the Sikamat returning officer, at the nomination centre at the Seremban City Council Forest Heights here today.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz will face Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Nor Azman Mohamad, while another contender is Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Datuk Razali Abu Samah, a former Melaka deputy police chief.
The Sikamat seat was previously held by Negri Sembilan PH chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, who is also the caretaker menteri besar.
However, Aminuddin has moved to contest the Linggi seat in this election.
The Ampangan seat will also see a three-cornered contest involving former Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Rafie Abd Malek, who is contesting as a PN candidate, PH's Datuk Muhammad Nazri Kassim and Noor'azah Harun of Bersatu.
During the 2018-2023 term, Rafie served as the Ampangan assemblyman under PH, but his name was dropped in the 15th state election, when he contested as an independent candidate.
In Lenggeng, BN's Datuk Mohd Asna Amin will defend the seat against Zarinna Abu Zarin of PH and Zool Amali Hussin of Bersatu.
Meanwhile, the Lobak seat will see a straight fight between PH's Chew Seh Yong and Dr P Kumar of PN.
The Nilai seat will see a five-cornered contest involving incumbent Arul Kumar Jambunathan of PH, Lai Chien Kong of BN, Bersatu's Datuk V Saravanan Kumar, Zamani Ibrahim of Parti Barisan Jemaah Islamiah Se-Malaysia (BERJASA) and independent candidate Omar Mohd Isa.
The Nilai seat is held by Arul Kumar Jambunathan, who is also a state executive councillor, after he won the seat with a majority of 10,889 votes in the 2023 state election.
The Temiang seat will see a three-cornered contest involving Ho Weng Wah of PH, BN's Leaw Kok Chan and Bersatu's Fazly Hamid.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jordan's army says shot down 10 Iranian missiles
Article:
AMMAN, (Jordan): Jordan's army said on Saturday it had shot down 10 Iranian missiles with no casualties or damage, as Teheran retaliated against American strikes by pressing attacks on US allies.
"Air defense systems... intercepted 10 Iranian missiles that had entered Jordanian airspace and were targeting the Kingdom's territory (which) were intercepted and shot down," the army said in a statement, adding that there were no casualties or material damage. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don’t turn adat into campaign fodder, Mohamad Hasan warns Negeri Sembilan BN party machinery
Article:
REMBAU, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has reminded the party’s election machinery not to use Negeri Sembilan’s adat (traditional customs) as a campaign issue in the 16th state election, saying the institution must be protected from political disputes.
Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said the state’s adat institution should be respected and kept separate from politics to prevent unnecessary tensions during the democratic process.
“I have made it clear and reminded everyone that during the election campaign, we must not touch on adat issues.
“We should not drag adat issues into politics, especially during campaigning, as this will only complicate the situation (in the state). There is no need to use adat issues as campaign material,” he told reporters after the nomination process at Dewan Seri Rembau today.
On cooperation between BN and Perikatan Nasional (PN), Mohamad said both coalitions would continue their electoral understanding to maximise voter support in constituencies where BN is not contesting.
He said the arrangement was aimed at helping candidates from both coalitions secure victories across all 36 state seats.
“We are maintaining an understanding, not forming a coalition as was seen in Johor. I hope that in Negeri Sembilan we will help one another to maximise support in the constituencies being contested,” he said.
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 5. The Election Commission has set July 28 for early voting and Aug 1 for polling.
For the latest updates on the state polls, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: Nilai turns five-way as DAP’s Arul Kumar faces crowded field
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 18 — Nilai assemblyman and DAP national vice-chairman J. Arul Kumar faces a tough challenge in a five-cornered contest in the upcoming 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.
The Nilai seat has drawn a crowded field, with incumbent Arul Kumar of Pakatan Harapan (PH) up against Zamani Ibrahim (Berjasa), Datuk Lai Chien Kong (Barisan Nasional), Datuk V. Saravana Kumar (Bersatu) and independent candidate Omar Mohd Isa.
The final list of candidates was announced by election returning officer Datuk Masri Baharuddin at the nomination centre in Wisma Bandaraya Seremban, shortly after the 10am nomination deadline.
Over in the Sikamat state seat, Nor Azman Mohamad, who serves as political secretary to the Menteri Besar, will carry PH’s flag in a three-way contest against Datuk Razali Abu Samah (Perikatan Nasional) and Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz (Bersatu).
The seat also initially attracted an independent candidate, Bujang Abu, but he withdrew from the race at the last minute.
Over in the Lenggeng seat, PH candidate Zarinna Abu Zarin will take on incumbent Datuk Mohd Asna Amin from BN, alongside Bersatu’s Zool Amali Hussin.
Meanwhile, Lobak is shaping up to be a straight one-on-one showdown between incumbent Chew Seh Yong (PH) and PN challenger Dr P. Kumar.
The Temiang seat, on the other hand, is set for a three-cornered tussle featuring PH’s Ho Weng Wah, political secretary to the Transport Minister, against Datuk Leaw Kok Chan (BN) and Fazly Hamid (Bersatu).
Over in Ampangan, it’s another three-way battle, with Yayasan Negeri Sembilan director Muhammad Nazri Kassim (PH) squaring off against Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Malek (PN) and Noor’azah Harun (Bersatu).
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly, which has 36 seats, was dissolved on June 5.
The Election Commission has set July 28 for early voting, with polling day scheduled for Aug 1.
For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi says Pakatan will let Tok Min’s track record do the talking as race to keep Negeri Sembilan begins
Article:
JEMPOL, July 18 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign for the Negeri Sembilan state election will centre on maintaining administrative continuity and highlighting Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s track record in leading the state since 2018.
PH Communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition would focus on Aminuddin’s leadership record and the achievements of the state administration, which he said had delivered positive economic outcomes for Negeri Sembilan.
“We can see that the performance and quality of the administration of Tok Min (Aminuddin) since helming Negeri Sembilan have shown excellent and encouraging achievements.
“They include increased zakat (tithe) collections, a surge in revenue collection for the state government and the consistent influx of foreign investments, including the construction of a new port,” he told reporters after the conclusion of the candidate nomination process for the Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang and Bahau state seats at the Jempol District and Land Office here.
Fahmi, who is also the Communications Minister, said PH would rely on the administration’s record to convince voters that continuity was important in ensuring continued economic stability and prosperity.
Commenting on PH’s strategy for the four-cornered contest in Jeram Padang, Fahmi said the coalition’s candidate would focus on employment opportunities, an issue frequently raised by young voters in the constituency.
He expressed confidence that PH candidate G. Manivannan could engage with the local community and offer new hope through the various economic opportunities being implemented and planned across the state.
Manivannan, a lawyer and political secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will face incumbent Datuk Mohd Zaidy Abdul Kadir of Barisan Nasional (BN), R. Sri Sanjeevan of Bersatu and Dayana Dal of Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli), the sole Orang Asli candidate.
Fahmi also reminded all candidates, party machinery and media practitioners to use the two-week campaign period responsibly and maintain harmony.
“As the Communications Minister, I hope all parties, particularly netizens, can campaign in a healthy manner. Avoid touching on sensitive matters involving the 3Rs (Religion, Race, Rulers) and spreading slander or fake news,” he said.
Fahmi said he would also monitor the welfare of media practitioners throughout the state election campaign.
The nomination process for the four state seats under the Jempol parliamentary constituency – Serting, Jeram Padang, Bahau and Palong – proceeded smoothly, with no untoward incidents reported.
Serting and Palong will see three-cornered contests, while Bahau will feature a straight fight between PH’s incumbent Teo Kok Seong of DAP and BN’s Chong Fui Ming of MCA.
In Serting, PH candidate Yaacob Mahmood will face incumbent Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Bersatu’s Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh @ Affendy Salleh.
In Palong, BN incumbent Datuk Mustapha Nagoor will be challenged by PH’s Muhammad Zahin Zinal Abidin and Bersatu’s Rebin Birham.
The Election Commission (EC) has set July 28 for early voting and Aug 1 as polling day for the Negeri Sembilan state election.
For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: A ‘50-50 seat’: Anthony Loke banks on Pakatan’s track record as he fights to retain mixed-majority Chennah
Article:
JELEBU, July 18 — The Chennah state seat is among the four most crucial constituencies in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency for Pakatan Harapan (PH) as it seeks to retain the Negeri Sembilan government in the upcoming state election, incumbent Anthony Loke said.
Loke, who is also the Transport Minister, said that although the contest is a straight fight, PH is confident of retaining the seat based on its track record of service and development over two terms in government.
“The PH government, which has been in power since 2018, has proven that we are capable of delivering progress and stability to the state.
“We hope to receive the people’s mandate to form the state government again, and the seats in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency are crucial because in 2018, two state seats here – Chennah and Kelawang – contributed to the formation of the state government,” he told reporters after the nomination process at Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang here today.
Earlier, Jelebu Parliamentary returning officer Abdul Rahim A Aziz announced that the Chennah state seat would witness a straight fight between Loke and Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Siow Kong Choon.
Commenting on his chances of retaining Chennah, a mixed-majority seat, following the recent Johor state election in which DAP lost several semi-urban constituencies, Loke said every election has its own dynamics.
“Chennah has always been a 50-50 seat because no single ethnic group forms the majority. To win, we need support across all communities.
“The difference this time is that we are contesting as the incumbent government, having served for two terms and delivered development to the area. I hope to retain the support of Chinese, Malay and Orang Asli voters,” he said.
Meanwhile, Loke said the nomination process had gone smoothly in all four state seats under the Jelebu parliamentary constituency, with PH candidates in Pertang, Sungai Lui and Kelawang facing three-cornered contests, while Chennah, which he is contesting, will see a straight fight.
He said PH was now fully focused on campaigning to retain voters’ support.
The Election Commission (EC) has set July 28 for early voting and Aug 1 as polling day for the Negeri Sembilan state election.
For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: Onn Hafiz kicks off second term with ‘no honeymoon’ warning for new Johor exco
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reminded the state’s newly appointed executive councillors (Exco) that there will be no “honeymoon” period, urging them instead to get straight to work in fulfilling their responsibilities to the people.
He said the strong and valuable mandate given by the people of Johor must be translated into harder work, more efficient public service delivery and tangible results that benefit all segments of society.
“Congratulations to everyone who has been appointed. This appointment is not a privilege. It is a great trust and an enormous responsibility. I have reminded the entire Exco line-up that we have no time for a honeymoon period. There is no honeymoon period.
“The state government’s priority now is to continue driving the Maju Johor 2030 agenda with greater focus and effectiveness to build a more developed, prosperous and inclusive Johor for all Johoreans,” he told a press conference at Saujana, the Johor Menteri Besar’s official residence, here today.
He also gave his assurance that the state government would continue to safeguard the interests of all communities regardless of their background to ensure the success of the Maju Johor 2030 agenda for the well-being of all Bangsa Johor.
Onn Hafiz said the new term would see continuity in the state administration, with six incumbent executive councillors retained and four new faces appointed to strengthen the state government machinery.
Earlier today, 10 assemblymen were sworn in as members of the Johor Exco before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, at Istana Bukit Serene here.
The six retained executive councillors are Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid, Ling Tian Soon, Lee Ting Han and Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh.
The four newly appointed executive councillors are Hasrunizah Hassan, Md Israk Abdullah, Dr Mohammad Naqib Md Ghazali and P. Pannir Selvam. — Bernama

Title: Zahid says BN-Perikatan tie-up is ‘understanding’, not pact, as coalition eyes Negeri Sembilan comeback
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today insisted his coalition’s electoral cooperation with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negeri Sembilan state election was an “understanding”, not a formal political pact.
Speaking after accompanying BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, who is defending the Rantau state seat, at the nomination centre in Rembau, Zahid said the tactical seat-sharing arrangement would help BN regain control of Negeri Sembilan.
“We want to ensure that this coordination is not an agreement or a pact, but an understanding.
“This understanding is very important to us because we previously won only 14 seats under BN.
“God willing, with this tactical approach, Negeri Sembilan will once again be governed by BN. The blue wave has arrived in Negeri Sembilan,” he said, according to
Berita Harian
.
BN is contesting 25 of the 36 state seats, with PN fielding candidates in the remaining 11 under the informal arrangement.
BN announced its first 25 candidates on Wednesday, with Mohamad saying the remaining seats had been left to “trusted friends”.
PN unveiled candidates for those constituencies the following day, comprising five from PAS, four from Parti Wawasan, and one each from Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP).
Zahid also urged BN’s election machinery to intensify efforts to secure a larger majority for Mohamad in Rantau, as well as victories for BN candidates contesting the Chembong and Kota state seats.
“With this new approach and renewed spirit, as the blue wave arrives, let us pray and work towards achieving an even larger majority.
“We respect and uphold our traditional institutions and the monarchy. God willing, Tok Mat (Mohamad), we are not breaking through the roof — we are building a new one,” he said.
Nomination for the Negeri Sembilan state election closed today, with polling set for August 1 and early voting on July 28. The election will decide all 36 seats in the Negeri Sembilan Legislative Assembly.

Title: Rantau showdown set as Umno veteran Tok Mat takes on young doctor from Pakatan
Article:
REMBAU, July 18 — The Rantau state seat is set for a straight fight in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, with state Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan facing Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi.
Returning officer Mohd Zamri Mohd Esa announced the contest after the nomination process, which began at 9am, concluded at Dewan Sri Rembau here today.
Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, has held the Rantau state seat since 2004.
Speaking to reporters afterwards, the Rembau Member of Parliament said BN’s focus throughout the campaign period would be to explain the coalition’s manifesto and convince voters to give BN the mandate on polling day.
“The nomination process has been completed, and all nomination papers have been accepted. The officially gazetted campaign period will now begin. I hope the BN machinery will carry out its campaign in a systematic and well-organised manner because good groundwork will produce good results,” he said.
Meanwhile, Dr Azizul Hakim said that despite facing a high-profile opponent, he is offering voters a different choice as a young professional candidate who will focus on healthcare initiatives and addressing local issues.
He said his 10 years of experience as a medical doctor, as well as his ownership of three clinics in Senawang, Puncak Alam and Melaka, have enabled him to better understand the community’s needs, particularly in healthcare.
“I want to serve the people of Rantau as a medical doctor. We want every resident of Rantau to enjoy good health with better access to healthcare services,” said the 35-year-old candidate.
In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan State Election, Mohamad secured 16,957 votes to defeat PH candidate Rozmal Malakan, who garnered 6,677 votes, giving him a 10,280-vote majority.
Meanwhile, the Paroi state seat will witness a three-cornered contest, with PH candidate Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah, the press secretary to the Menteri Besar, facing Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Kamarol Ridzuan Mohd Zin and Mohd Nazree Mohd Yunus of Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu).
In the Chembong state seat, incumbent and BN candidate Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris will face a straight fight against PH candidate Danish Nazran Murad.
Meanwhile, the Kota state seat will see a three-cornered contest, with incumbent Suhaimi Aini from BN taking on PH candidate Muhammad Allif Ibrahim and Bersatu candidate Akmal Noradzmi Abdul Rahim.
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly, which has 36 seats, was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission (EC) setting July 28 as early voting day and Aug 1 as polling day.
A total of 889,490 registered voters are eligible to cast their ballots in the state election, comprising 867,151 ordinary voters, 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 police personnel as early voters.
For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: One seat, four candidates: Jeram Padang stands out in Negeri Sembilan polls as only four-way clash
Article:
JEMPOL, July 18 — Jeram Padang is the only seat in the Jempol state constituency to witness a four-cornered contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, with Serting and Palong seeing three-cornered contests and Bahau set for a straight fight.
Returning officer Amino Agos Suyub announced the four-cornered contest in Jeram Padang after the nomination process at the Jempol District and Land Office Hall ended at 10am today.
The Jeram Padang state seat will see Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate G. Manivannan go up against incumbent Datuk Mohd Zaidy Abdul Kadir of Barisan Nasional (BN), R. Sri Sanjeevan of Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and Dayana Dal of Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI), the only Orang Asli candidate vying for the seat.
